modules.name: MoleculeACE.models.attentivefp
documentation:
Author: Derek van Tilborg -- TU/e -- 23-05-2022

Attentive Fingerprint [1] graph neural network for regression.

[1] Xiong et al. (2020). Pushing the Boundaries of Molecular Representation for Drug Discovery with the Graph Attention
Mechanism

modules.name: MoleculeACE.models.transformer
documentation:
Author: Derek van Tilborg -- TU/e -- 23-05-2022

Functions to run a ChemBerta transformer for bioactivity prediction. We used pre-trained weights of ChemBerta trained
on 10 million SMILES strings from PubChem.

    - RobertaRegressionHead:    regression head that is slapped on the frozen pre-trained model
    - Transformer:              class of Transformer model
        - train()
        - test()
        - predict()
    - ChemBertaDataset:         dataset class to work with the Transformer model class
    - chemberta_loader():       function that returns a dataloader

classes.name: MoleculeACE.models.transformer.RobertaRegressionHead
documentation: Head for singletask regression models.

methods.name: MoleculeACE.models.transformer.Transformer.__init__
documentation:
---
param batch_size: (int) batch_size
param epochs: (int) train for n epochs (is the default for train() if no epoch argument
  is given there)
param freeze_core: (bool) Freeze the core of the transformer (aka only train the regression
  head)
param lr: (float) learning rate
param save_path: (str) path where intermediate models are stored
---

methods.name: MoleculeACE.models.transformer.Transformer.train
documentation:
---
param early_stopping_patience: (int) stop training if the model doesn't improve after
  n epochs
param epochs: (int) epochs to train
param print_every_n: (int) printout training progress every nth epoch
param x_train: (Dict[Tensor, Tensor]) dict from ChemBerta tokenizer with train data
param x_val: (Dict[Tensor, Tensor]) dict from ChemBerta tokenizer with val data
param y_train: '(List[float]): train bioactivity'
param y_val: '(List[float]): validation bioactivity'
---

methods.name: MoleculeACE.models.transformer.Transformer.test
documentation:
Perform testing

```
param data_loader

Torch geometric data loader with test data

return

A tuple of two 1D-tensors (predicted, true)
```

methods.name: MoleculeACE.models.transformer.Transformer.predict
documentation:
Perform prediction

```
param x_test

dict from the ChemBerta Tokenizer

return

A 1D-tensor with predicted bioactivities
```

methods.name: MoleculeACE.models.transformer.ChemBertaDataset.__init__
documentation: Create a dataset for the ChemBerta transformer using a pretrained tokenizer

modules.name: MoleculeACE.models.gat
documentation:
Author: Derek van Tilborg -- TU/e -- 23-05-2022

Basic graph attention network [1] using a transformer as global pooling [2].

1. Veličković et al. (2018). Graph Attention Networks
2. Baek et al. (2021). Accurate Learning of Graph Representations with Graph Multiset Pooling

modules.name: MoleculeACE.models.gcn
documentation:
Author: Derek van Tilborg -- TU/e -- 19-05-2022

Basic graph convolutional network [1] using a transformer as global pooling [2].

1. Kipf & Welling (2017). Semi-Supervised Classification with Graph Convolutional Networks
2. Baek et al. (2021). Accurate Learning of Graph Representations with Graph Multiset Pooling

modules.name: MoleculeACE.models.utils
documentation:
Author: Derek van Tilborg -- TU/e -- 23-05-2022

Utility functions that are used by several different models.

    - GNN():                 Base GNN parent class for all graph neural network models
    - NN():                  Base NN parent class for all basic neural network models (MLP and CNN)
    - graphs_to_loader():    Turn lists of molecular graphs and their bioactivities into a dataloader
    - plot_loss():           plot the losses of Torch models
    - scatter():             scatter plot of true/predicted for a Torch model using a dataloader
    - numpy_loader():        simple Torch dataloader from numpy arrays
    - squeeze_if_needed():   if the input is a squeezable tensor, squeeze it

functions.name: MoleculeACE.models.utils.graphs_to_loader
documentation: Turn a list of graph objects and a list of labels into a Dataloader


modules.name: MoleculeACE.models.mpnn
documentation:
Author: Derek van Tilborg -- TU/e -- 23-05-2022

Message Passing Neural Network for molecular property prediction using continuous kernel-based convolution
(edge-conditioned convolution) [1] and global graph pooling using a graph multiset transformer [2] instead of
the Set2Set method used in [1]. I added dropout to make a more robust

[1] Gilmer et al. (2017). Neural Message Passing for Quantum Chemistry
[2] Baek et al. (2021). Accurate Learning of Graph Representations with Graph Multiset Pooling

modules.name: MoleculeACE.models.cnn
documentation:
Author: Derek van Tilborg -- TU/e -- 24-05-2022

Basic 1-D Convolutional Neural Network based on the architecture of [1]

[1] Kimber et al. (2021). Maxsmi: Maximizing molecular property prediction performance with confidence estimation using
    SMILES augmentation and deep learning

  = 41)
param seq_len_in: (int) length of the SMILES sequence
---

modules.name: MoleculeACE.models.lstm
documentation:
Author: Derek van Tilborg -- TU/e -- June 2022

Inspired by:

Moret, M., Grisoni, F., Katzberger, P. & Schneider, G.
Perplexity-based molecule ranking and bias estimation of chemical language models.
ChemRxiv (2021) doi:10.26434/chemrxiv-2021-zv6f1-v2.

    - LSTM:                         LSTM model class
        - train()
        - test()
        - predict()
    - LSTMNextToken:                LSTM autoregressive model for pre-training
        - train()
        - test()
        - predict()
    - DataGeneratorNextToken:       Generator class for the next-token model
    - DataGeneratorRegression:      Generator class for the regression LSTM

modules.name: MoleculeACE.models.ml
documentation:
Author: Derek van Tilborg -- TU/e -- 23-05-2022

A collection of classical machine learning algorithms

    -MLmodel:       parent class used by all machine learning algorithms
    -RF:            Random Forest Regressor
    -GBM:           Gradient Boosting Regressor
    -SVM:           Support Vector Regressor
    -KNN:           K-Nearest neighbour Regressor

modules.name: MoleculeACE.models.optimize
documentation:
Author: Derek van Tilborg -- TU/e -- 27-05-2022

Functions for Bayesian hyperparameter optimization with cross-validation

    - BayesianOptimization:             Class that does all the heavy lifting for Bayesian optimization
        - optimize()
        - plot_progress()
        - history_to_csv()
        - save_config()
    - dict_to_search_space():           Transforms a dict of config settings to a hyperparameter searchspace
    - count_hyperparam_combinations()   Checks if the number of optimization tries is not exceeding whats needed
    - cross_validate()                  function to perform n-fold cross validation

methods.name: MoleculeACE.models.optimize.BayesianOptimization.__init__
documentation:
Init the class with a trainable model. The model class should contain a train(), test(), predict() function
and be initialized with its hyperparameters

documentation: Function to perform outlier analysis and compute descriptive metrics
Francesca Grisoni, TU/e April 2021

---
param alpha: true
param convert_to_log: true

return: true
---

functions.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.data_fetching.exp_property_curation._InitLookUp
documentation:
Initialize the dictionary of the look up table based on the confidence level. The tabulated data are from
Rorabacher, D.B.(1991) Analytical Chemistry 63(2), 139–46.

```
param alpha

confidence interval (double), accepted values: 0.1, 0.05, 0.01

return

```

functions.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.data_fetching.exp_property_curation.dixon_test
documentation:
Keyword arguments:
    data = A ordered or unordered list of data points (int or float).
    left = Q-test of minimum value in the ordered list if True.
    right = Q-test of maximum value in the ordered list if True.
    q_dict = A dictionary of Q-values for a given confidence level,
        where the dict. keys are sample sizes N, and the associated values
        are the corresponding critical Q values. E.g.,
        {3: 0.97, 4: 0.829, 5: 0.71, 6: 0.625, ...}

Returns a list of two values for the outliers, or None.
E.g.,
   for [1,1,1] -> [None, None]
   for [5,1,1] -> [None, 5]
   for [5,1,5] -> [1, None]
# inspired by https://sebastianraschka.com/Articles/2014_dixon_test.html

functions.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.data_fetching.exp_property_curation.compute_stats
documentation:
computes mean, standard deviation and standard error of the mean of the input array values
\:param values: array with values
\:return: num_values (double): number of values in the array

> mean (double): average
> std (double): standard deviation
> sem (double): standard error of the mean
==== other useful functions

\:param outlier:
\:param data:
\:param outliers_index:
\:return:

modules.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.cliffs
documentation:
Author: Derek van Tilborg -- TU/e -- 25-05-2022

Code to compute activity cliffs

    - ActivityCliffs:                   Class that takes care of computing activity cliffs
    - find_fc():                        Calculate the fold change
    - get_fc():                         Compute the pairwise fold change
    - get_levenshtein_matrix():         Compute the pairwise Levenshtein similarity
    - get_tanimoto_matrix():            Compute the pairwise Tanimoto similarity
    - get_scaffold_matrix():            Compute the pairwise scaffold similarity
    - get_mmp_matrix():                 Compute a matrix of Matched Molecular Pairs
    - mmp_similarity():                 Compute binary mmp similarity matrix
    - moleculeace_similarity():         Compute the consensus similarity (being >0.9 in at least one similarity type)

functions.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.cliffs.find_fc
documentation: Get the fold change of to bioactivities

functions.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.cliffs.get_fc
documentation: Calculates the pairwise fold difference in compound activity given a list of activities

functions.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.cliffs.get_levenshtein_matrix
documentation: Calculates a matrix of levenshtein similarity scores for a list of SMILES string

functions.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.cliffs.get_tanimoto_matrix
documentation: Calculates a matrix of Tanimoto similarity scores for a list of SMILES string

functions.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.cliffs.get_scaffold_matrix
documentation: Calculates a matrix of Tanimoto similarity scores for a list of SMILES string

functions.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.cliffs.find_fragments
documentation:
Build a database of molecular fragments for matched molecular pair analysis. Molecular fragmentation from the
Hussain and Rae algorithm is used. We only use a single cut (true to the original MMP idea)

functions.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.cliffs.mmp_match
documentation: Check if fragments (provided as rdkit Mol objects are substructures of another molecule (from SMILES string)

functions.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.cliffs.get_mmp_matrix
documentation: Calculates a matrix of matched molecular pairs for a list of SMILES string

functions.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.cliffs.mmp_similarity
documentation: Calculate which pairs of molecules are matched molecular pairs

functions.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.cliffs.moleculeace_similarity
documentation: Calculate which pairs of molecules have a high tanimoto, scaffold, or SMILES similarity

functions.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.cliffs.is_cliff
documentation: Calculates if two molecules are activity cliffs

classes.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.cliffs.ActivityCliffs

methods.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.cliffs.ActivityCliffs.find_cliffs
documentation:
Compute activity cliffs

```
param similarity

(float) threshold value to determine structural similarity

param potency_fold

(float) threshold value to determine difference in bioactivity

param custom_cliff_function

(Callable) function that takes: smiles: List[str] and similarity: float and
returns a square binary matrix where 1 is similar and 0 is not.

param mmp

(bool) use matched molecular pairs to determine similarity instead
```

methods.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.cliffs.ActivityCliffs.get_cliff_molecules
documentation:
---
param kwargs: arguments for ActivityCliffs.find_cliffs()
param return_smiles: (bool) return activity cliff molecules as a list of SMILES strings
return: (List[int]) returns a binary list where 1 means activity cliff compounds
---
Activity cliff class that computes cliff compounds

modules.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.data_prep
documentation:
Author: Derek van Tilborg -- TU/e -- 22-05-2022

A collection of data-prepping functions
    - split_data():             split ChEMBL csv into train/test taking similarity and cliffs into account. If you want
                                to process your own data, use this function
    - process_data():           see split_data()
    - load_data():              load a pre-processed dataset from the benchmark
    - fetch_data():             download molecular bioactivity data from ChEMBL for a specific drug target

functions.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.data_prep.split_data
documentation:
Split data into train/test according to activity cliffs and compounds characteristics.

```
param smiles

(List[str]) list of SMILES strings

param bioactivity

(List[float]) list of bioactivity values

param n_clusters

(int) number of clusters the data is split into for getting homogeneous data splits

param test_size

(float) test split

param similarity

(float) similarity threshold for calculating activity cliffs

param potency_fold

(float) potency difference threshold for calculating activity cliffs

param remove_stereo

(bool) Remove racemic mixtures altogether?

return

df[smiles, exp_mean [nM], y, cliff_mol, split]
```

functions.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.data_prep.process_data
documentation:
Split data into train/test according to activity cliffs and compounds characteristics.

```
param smiles

(List[str]) list of SMILES strings

param bioactivity

(List[float]) list of bioactivity values

param n_clusters

(int) number of clusters the data is split into for getting homogeneous data splits

param test_size

(float) test split

param similarity

(float) similarity threshold for calculating activity cliffs

param potency_fold

(float) potency difference threshold for calculating activity cliffs

param remove_stereo

(bool) Remove racemic mixtures altogether?

return

df[smiles, exp_mean [nM], y, cliff_mol, split]
```

functions.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.data_prep.fetch_data
documentation: Download and prep the data from CHEMBL. Throws out duplicates, problematic molecules, and extreme outliers

functions.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.data_prep.find_stereochemical_siblings
documentation:
Detects molecules that have different SMILES strings, but ecode for the same molecule with
different stereochemistry. For racemic mixtures it is often unclear which one is measured/active

Args:
    smiles: (lst) list of SMILES strings

Returns: (lst) List of SMILES having a similar molecule with different stereochemistry

modules.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.utils
documentation:
Author: Derek van Tilborg -- TU/e -- 23-05-2022

A collection of random functions that are used in different places

    - Data:                     Class that holds all data, featurizes it, and can augment it
        - featurize_data()
        - shuffle()
        - augment()
    - get_config():             function to load a dict of parameters from a .yml or .json
    - write_config():           function to write a dict of parameters to a .yml
    - calc_rmse():              calculate Root Mean Square Error
    - calc_cliff_rmse():        calculate Root Mean Square Error on activity cliff compounds
    - cross_validate():         cross-validates a model and calculates RMSE and RMSE_cliff for each fold
    - smi_tokenizer():          convert a SMILES string into character tokens
    - augment():                add non-canonical SMILES strings and manage the labels accordingly
    - random_smiles():          create a single random non-canonical SMILES string
    - smile_augmentation():     create n alternative SMILES strings

functions.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.utils.load_model
documentation: Load a model

functions.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.utils.get_config
documentation: Load a yml config file

functions.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.utils.write_config
documentation: Write a dictionary to a .yml file

functions.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.utils.get_benchmark_config
documentation:
Get the default configs

```
param dataset

(str) one of the 30 datasets included in MoleculeACE

param algorithm

(str) Algorithm class or name of one of the supported algorithms. Pick from:
['RF', 'SVM', 'GBM', 'KNN', 'MLP', 'GCN', 'MPNN', 'AFP', 'GAT', 'CNN', 'LSTM', 'Transformer']

param descriptor

(str) Descriptor class or name of one of the supported descriptors. Pick from:
['ECFP', 'MACCS', 'PHYSCHEM', 'WHIM', 'GRAPH', 'SMILES', 'TOKENS']

return

(dict) hyperparameters
```

functions.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.utils.calc_rmse
documentation:
Calculates the Root Mean Square Error

Args:
    true: (1d array-like shape) true test values (float)
    pred: (1d array-like shape) predicted test values (float)

Returns: (float) rmse

functions.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.utils.calc_cliff_rmse
documentation:
Calculate the RMSE of activity cliff compounds

```
param y_test_pred

(lst/array) predicted test values

param y_test

(lst/array) true test values

param cliff_mols_test

(lst) binary list denoting if a molecule is an activity cliff compound

param smiles_test

(lst) list of SMILES strings of the test molecules

param y_train

(lst/array) train labels

param smiles_train

(lst) list of SMILES strings of the train molecules

param kwargs

arguments for ActivityCliffs()

return

float RMSE on activity cliff compounds
```

functions.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.utils.cross_validate
documentation:
---
param data: Moleculace.benchmark.utils.Data object
param early_stopping: (int) stop training when not making progress for n epochs
param hyperparameters: '(dict) dict of hyperparameters {name_of_param: value}'
param model: a model that has a train(), test(), and predict() method and is initialized
  with its hyperparameters
param n_folds: (int) n folds for cross-validation
param save_path: (str) path to save trained models
param seed: (int) random seed
return: (list) rmse_scores, (list) cliff_rmse_scores
---

modules.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.featurization
documentation:
Author: Derek van Tilborg -- TU/e -- 20-05-2022

All code for featurizing a molecules into molecular descriptors, molecular graphs, one-hot endode SMILES strings and
tokens.

    - Featurizer:                   Main molecule featurizer class that converts SMILES strings to computable info.
                                        example:    x_train = Featurizer().ecfp(list_of_smiles)
        - ecfp()
        - maccs()
        - whim()
        - physchem()
        - one_hot()
        - tokens()
        - graphs()

    - OneHotEncodeSMILES:           Class that can one-hot encode SMILES strings
    - featurize_graph():            Construct a molecular graph from a SMILES string
    - compute_whim():               Computes WHIM descriptors from a SMILES string
    - compute_physchem():           Computes physical-chemical properties a SMILES string

functions.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.featurization.mols_from_smiles
documentation: Create a list of RDkit mol objects from a list of SMILES strings

functions.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.featurization.rdkit_numpy_convert
documentation: Convert a RDkit fingerprint object to simple numpy array

functions.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.featurization.sigmoid
documentation: numerically semi-stable sigmoid function to map charge between 0 and 1

functions.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.featurization.molecule_from_smiles
documentation: Sanitize a molecule from a SMILES string

documentation: Convert SMILES to ECFP fingerprints

methods.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.featurization.Featurizer.maccs
documentation: Convert SMILES to MACCs fingerprints

methods.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.featurization.Featurizer.whim
documentation: Compute WHIM descriptors from SMILES

methods.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.featurization.Featurizer.physchem

methods.name: MoleculeACE.benchmark.featurization.Featurizer.tokens
documentation:
Tokenize SMILES for a ChemBerta Transformer

```
param max_smiles_length

(int) Maximal allowable SMILES string length

param padding

(bool) allow padding

param truncation

(bool) allow truncation (you will need this for heterogeneous SMILES strings)

param auto_tokenizer

(str) name of the auto tokenizer provided by HuggingFace

return

Dict['input_ids': tensor, 'attention_mask': tensor], tensors are of shape N x max_smiles_length
```

this will turn a list of smiles in string format
